NOTICE OF HEARING FOR PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, APPENDIX, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT KELLER, KRIEG, SLOAN, REILLEY & ROMAN LLP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY PARTNERSHIP HEARING SET FOR DEC-18-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KELLER, KRIEG, SLOAN, REILLEY & ROMAN LLP'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION GRANTED. THIS ACTION IS STAYED PENDING ARBITRATION. THE AGREEMENT SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIES WITH THE FACTORS SET FORTH IN ARMENDARIZ VS. FOUNDATION HEALTH PSYCHARE SERVICES, INC. (2000) 24 CAL. 4TH 83. THE COST-SHARING PROVISION IS UNLAWFUL, BUT THE AGREEMENT IS NOT SO PERMEATED WITH UNCONSCIONABLE PROVISIONS THAT IT CANNOT BE SAVED. THE COST-SHARING PROVISION IS SEVERED FROM THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. (SEE MCMANUS VS. CIBC WORLD MARKETS CORP. (2003) 109 CAL.APP. 4TH 76.) THE AGREEMENT PROVIDES FOR A NEUTRAL ARBITRATOR AND DOES NOT LIMIT PLAINTIFF'S REMEDIES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR #10276
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-18-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Notice Of Hearing For Petition To Compel Arbitration, Appendix
Off Calendar Dec-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS KELLER, KRIEG, SLOAN, REILLEY & ROMAN LLP'S Petition to Compel Arbitration. Appearances: Mark C. Thomas, Esq. of Brownstein Thomas LLP (415) 986-1338 , appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; G. Daniel Newland, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP (415) 397-2823 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition granted. This action is stayed pending arbitration. The agreement substantially complies with the factors set forth in Armendariz vs. Foundation Health Psychare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal. 4th 83. The cost-sharing provision is unlawful, but the agreement is not so permeated with unconscionable provisions that it cannot be saved. The cost-sharing provision is severed from the arbitration agreement. (See McManus vs. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App. 4th 76.) The agreement provides for a neutral arbitrator and does not limit plaintiff's remedies. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Jacqueline Chan #10276
